design_principles: Each use case is one atomic system action — one transaction boundary
Use cases orchestrate domain behavior — they never implement domain rules or invariants directly
Use cases load domain objects, invoke behavioral methods, persist results, drain events, return response
Cross-context calls go exclusively through ports defined in the caller's application layer
No domain object from one bounded context is passed into another — only primitive DTOs cross boundaries

LangGraph graphs (TurnGraph, DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph) are the execution engines wired inside their parent use case — the use case is the entry point and transaction owner; the graph is materialized in Task 2 (Workflow Orchestration)
Commands carry only the data needed to execute — no domain objects, no infrastructure references

use_cases.name: StartSessionUseCase
use_cases.purpose: Creates and fully initializes a ConversationSession for a user. Enforces one-session-per-user constraint. Loads all identity and history data from DB. Pre-seeds DecisionContext in COMP-2 via port. Transitions session to idle state ready for the first turn.

fields.name: user_id
fields.note: Pre-resolved trusted user identity. Never authenticated here.

fields.name: status
fields.note: idle

dependencies.role: load User by user_id
load School by user_id
load prior ConversationHistory by user_id
load PurchaseHistory by user_id (optional — empty list if none)
find_active_by_user_id (INV-CS-7 guard), save session
pre_seed_context: sends PreSeedContextDTO to COMP-2

execution_flow: 1. Validate user_id is not empty
2. Call IConversationSessionRepository.find_active_by_user_id(user_id) — if active or idle session already exists, raise ConflictError (enforces INV-CS-7)
3. Call IUserRepository.find_by_id(user_id) — raise NotFoundError if user does not exist (enforces INV-CS-6)
4. Call ISchoolRepository.find_by_user_id(user_id) — raise NotFoundError if school not found (enforces INV-CS-3)
5. Call IConversationHistoryRepository.find_by_user_id(user_id) — returns empty list gracefully if no history
6. Call IPurchaseHistoryRepository.find_by_user_id(user_id) — returns empty list gracefully if no records
7. Call ConversationSession.create(user_id, school_id) — session enters created state
8. Call session.begin_initialization() — session transitions created → initializing
9. Build PreSeedContextDTO(session_id, school_id, school_name, purchase_signals) from loaded data
10. Call IDecisionIntelligenceService.pre_seed_context(PreSeedContextDTO) — COMP-2 creates DecisionContext with school anchor (enforces INV-CS-3, INV-CS-5)
11. Call session.complete_initialization() — session transitions initializing → idle; emits SessionStarted
12. Call IConversationSessionRepository.save(session)
13. Drain domain events (SessionStarted — consumed by observability handlers)
14. Return StartSessionResponse(session_id, status='idle')
invariants_enforced: INV-CS-3 — school pre-seeded into DecisionContext before session becomes active
INV-CS-5 — DecisionContext exists before session enters active state
INV-CS-6 — user_id is pre-resolved; raise if user not found
INV-CS-7 — one active/idle session per user; reject if already exists
use_cases.notes: ConversationHistory is loaded here to pass as context to the pre-seeding step if needed. PurchaseHistory signals are forwarded to COMP-2 as raw strings — no domain object crosses the boundary.

use_cases.name: ProcessTurnUseCase
use_cases.trigger_workflow: WF0 — per-turn execution (classifies intent, retrieves evidence, generates and evaluates response)
use_cases.purpose: Entry point for every conversation turn. Guards session state. Initializes and runs TurnGraph (LangGraph execution engine). TurnGraph orchestrates intent classification, decision intelligence delegation, response generation, and response evaluation. Persists the completed turn. Returns the validated AssistantResponse to the API layer.

fields.name: user_id
fields.note: Passed through for TurnGraph state initialization and history persistence

fields.name: message
fields.note: Raw user message text

fields.name: context_precision_score
fields.note: 0.0 if no retrieval occurred

fields.name: context_recall_score
fields.note: 0.0 if no retrieval occurred

dependencies.role: load session, save session after turn completes
load full ConversationHistory before graph invocation (to populate conversation_history: list[dict]); append user message and assistant response entries after turn
load User.name as user_name before TurnGraph invocation for LLM personalization
load School.name as school_name before TurnGraph invocation for LLM personalization
classify Intent from user message (TurnGraph node)
process_turn: sends TurnInputDTO, receives TurnIntelligenceResultDTO (TurnGraph node)
generate AssistantResponse grounded in retrieved evidence (TurnGraph node)
evaluate groundedness_score and relevance_score on response (TurnGraph node)
persist per-turn RAG evaluation metrics after TurnGraph completes

execution_flow: 1. Call IConversationSessionRepository.find_by_id(session_id) — raise NotFoundError if not found
2. Guard: session must be in idle state; raise InvalidStateError if in initializing, created, or closed state (enforces INV-CS-4)
3. Guard: session.user_id must match command.user_id — raise SessionOwnershipError if mismatch (enforces INV-CS-5)

4. Load full ConversationHistory via IConversationHistoryRepository.find_by_user_id(user_id). Map to conversation_history: list[dict] with {"role": "user"|"assistant", "content": str} pairs (Proposal B — explicit history injection threaded through all LLM calls).
4a. Call IUserRepository.find_by_id(user_id) — load User.name as user_name (default '' if not found).

4b. Call ISchoolRepository.find_by_user_id(user_id) — load School.name as school_name (default '' if not found). Both user_name and school_name are injected into TurnGraphState so all LLM response nodes (N3 generate_response, N4 generate_clarification, N8 handle_out_of_scope) can personalise their system prompts via OpenAIResponseGenerator.
5. Call session.receive_message() — transitions idle → active
6. Open try block covering steps 7–12:
7. Build initial TurnGraphState(session_id, user_id, message, turn_number=session.turn_count+1, conversation_history, user_name, school_name)
8. Run TurnGraph — execution engine for the full turn pipeline (see TurnGraph in Workflow Orchestration task):
8a. classify_intent node: calls IIntentClassifier.classify(message, conversation_history) → Intent
8b. Conditional routing on intent:
- out_of_scope → handle_out_of_scope node (polite scope-redirect; DI and retrieval bypassed; history not persisted)
- ambiguous → generate_clarification node
- general_question → generate_redirect node (redirect; DI and retrieval bypassed)
- product_inquiry / referral_question / clarification_response → process_decision_intelligence node
8c. process_decision_intelligence node: calls IDecisionIntelligenceService.process_turn(TurnInputDTO(session_id, intent, message, turn_number, conversation_history)) → TurnIntelligenceResultDTO
— if TurnIntelligenceResultDTO.clarification_needed: route to generate_clarification node
8d. generate_response node: calls IResponseGenerator.generate(message, evidence, context_snapshot) → AssistantResponse (context_snapshot carries user_name and school_name)
8e. route_after_generate_response: general_question → persist_turn (no evidence; Fix 5); RAG intents → evaluate_response node
8f. evaluate_response node: calls IResponseEvaluator.evaluate(response, intent, evidence) → ScoredAssistantResponse
— scores pass: route to persist_turn node
— scores fail: route to return_fallback node → AssistantResponse.fallback()
8g. generate_clarification node: routes directly to persist_turn (Fix 4 — no evidence to evaluate)
9. Call session.complete_turn(intent, response) — transitions active → idle; emits TurnProcessed; increments turn_count

10. On any exception raised in steps 7–9: call session.fail_turn() — transitions active → idle (rollback, no event emitted); call IConversationSessionRepository.save(session); re-raise the exception so the API layer returns the correct HTTP error.
11. Append ConversationHistory entries: user message and assistant response
12. Call IConversationSessionRepository.save(session)
13. Call IConversationHistoryRepository.append() for both history entries
14. Drain domain events (TurnProcessed)
15. Return ProcessTurnResponse from final TurnGraph state
invariants_enforced: INV-CS-4 — turn rejected if session is not in idle state
INV-WF-1 — turn is persisted to history after completion
INV-AR-1 — groundedness_score must reach threshold (enforced inside TurnGraph evaluate_response node)
INV-AR-5 — response blocked and fallback returned if scores fail (TurnGraph evaluate_response conditional edge)
INV-AR-6 — relevance_score computed via IResponseEvaluator (reverse-question technique)

cross_context_calls.dto_in: TurnIntelligenceResultDTO(evidence_chunks, evidence_type, context_recall_score, context_precision_score, context_state, clarification_needed, clarification_question)

use_cases.notes: The TurnGraph is the execution engine — it is initialized and run here but its node/edge definitions are materialized in the Workflow Orchestration task (Task 2). This use case is the owner of the transaction boundary: it begins with session.receive_message() and closes with session.complete_turn() and persistence.

use_cases.name: CloseSessionUseCase
use_cases.purpose: Transitions a ConversationSession to the closed terminal state. Enforces that only idle sessions can be closed. Persists the final session state.

fields.name: user_id
fields.note: Used to verify session ownership before closing

fields.name: status
fields.note: closed

dependencies.role: load session, save terminal state

execution_flow: 1. Call IConversationSessionRepository.find_by_id(session_id) — raise NotFoundError if not found
2. Guard: session.user_id must match command.user_id — raise AuthorizationError if mismatch
3. Call session.close() — domain guard enforces status == idle; transitions idle → closed; emits SessionClosed (illegal if session is in active state — enforces INV-CS-2)
4. Call IConversationSessionRepository.save(session)
5. Drain domain events (SessionClosed)
6. Return CloseSessionResponse(session_id, status='closed')
invariants_enforced: INV-CS-2 — only idle sessions can be closed (active → closed is an illegal transition; domain guard raises DomainError)
use_cases.notes: The domain's close() method guard enforces the transition rule. This use case does not need to check status explicitly — it delegates that responsibility to the domain.

use_cases.name: PreSeedContextUseCase
use_cases.trigger_workflow: WF0 — session init sequence, called by COMM-1 (IDecisionIntelligenceService.pre_seed_context)
use_cases.purpose: Creates and pre-seeds a DecisionContext for a new session. Sets the school anchor as the permanent fundraising reference. Optionally converts PurchaseHistory signals into early PreferenceSignals. Called once per session before any turn.

fields.name: purchase_signals
fields.note: Raw purchase signal strings from PurchaseHistory. Empty list if user has no history.

output_response.name: None
output_response.note: Returns None (void). Called for its side effect of creating and persisting the DecisionContext.

dependencies.role: save newly created DecisionContext

execution_flow: 1. Build SchoolAnchor(school_id, school_name) value object
2. Call DecisionContext.create(session_id, school_anchor) — context enters empty state with school pre-seeded (enforces INV-DC-2)

3. If purchase_signals is non-empty: for each raw signal string, create PreferenceSignal(key='purchase_history', value=signal, turn_number=0) and call context.add_signal(signal) — context may transition empty → partial
4. Call IDecisionContextRepository.save(context)
5. Return None
invariants_enforced: INV-DC-2 — school_anchor is always present; DecisionContext.create() raises if null
INV-CS-3 — school anchor is set before any turn begins (enforced via this use case being called from StartSessionUseCase)
use_cases.notes: This use case is invoked indirectly by COMP-1 through IDecisionIntelligenceService.pre_seed_context() → DecisionIntelligenceAdapter (shell) → this use case. COMP-1 never imports this use case directly.

use_cases.name: ProcessTurnIntelligenceUseCase
use_cases.trigger_workflow: WF1 (Decision Context Building) + WF2 (Product Recommendation) + WF3 (Referral Guidance) — executed per turn
use_cases.purpose: Entry point for COMP-2 per conversation turn. Initializes and runs DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph (LangGraph execution engine). The subgraph extracts preference signals, updates DecisionContext, evaluates context readiness, constructs the retrieval query, executes retrieval via COMP-3 port, and evaluates evidence quality. Returns TurnIntelligenceResultDTO to COMP-1 (including clarification signal if gaps are detected).

fields.name: intent
fields.note: Intent value as string — not as BC-1 domain object

fields.name: message
fields.note: Raw user message text

fields.name: turn_number
fields.note: Current turn count from ConversationSession — used for PreferenceSignal timestamping

fields.name: conversation_history
fields.note: Full conversation history as OpenAI-format messages [{"role": "user"|"assistant", "content": str}]. Loaded once in ProcessTurnUseCase and threaded through all LLM calls so every model sees full conversational context.

output_response.name: TurnIntelligenceResult
output_response.note: Mapped to TurnIntelligenceResultDTO by DecisionIntelligenceAdapter (shell) before reaching COMP-1

fields.name: evidence_chunks
fields.note: Retrieved content text chunks. Empty if clarification needed.

fields.name: evidence_type
fields.note: product_evidence | referral_rule_evidence | none

fields.name: context_recall_score
fields.note: 0.0 if no retrieval occurred

fields.name: context_precision_score
fields.note: 0.0 if no retrieval occurred

fields.name: context_state
fields.note: Current DecisionContext state: empty|partial|ready|enriched

fields.name: clarification_question
fields.note: Suggested clarification question if gaps detected

fields.name: school_name
fields.note: Forwarded from SchoolAnchor for LLM personalization in BC-1. Default empty string.

dependencies.role: load DecisionContext by session_id, save after update
extract PreferenceSignals from message (subgraph node)
compute context_recall_score and context_precision_score on retrieved evidence (subgraph node)
retrieve evidence from COMP-3 via RetrievalQueryDTO (subgraph node)

execution_flow: 1. Call IDecisionContextRepository.find_by_session_id(session_id) — raise NotFoundError if context not found (context must have been pre-seeded)
2. Build initial DecisionGraphState(session_id, intent, message, turn_number, conversation_history, context_snapshot=context.snapshot())
3. Run DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph — execution engine for the full decision pipeline (see DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph in Workflow Orchestration task):

3a. extract_signals node: calls ISignalExtractor.extract(message, turn_number, conversation_history) → list[PreferenceSignal] (full conversation history provides context for interpreting all user messages including clarification answers)
3b. update_decision_context node: calls context.add_signal() for each extracted signal; transitions context state if thresholds met
3c. evaluate_context_readiness node: calls context.record_gap_evaluation(gaps); if gaps non-empty → emits GapDetected; calls context.mark_ready() if context is sufficient
3d. Conditional routing on context readiness:

- intent == 'clarification_response' AND context.pending_retrieval_intent is set AND gaps resolved → override state intent to pending_retrieval_intent, clear_pending_retrieval_intent(), route to construct_retrieval_query (Fix 2 — resume original retrieval flow)

- intent == 'clarification_response' AND context.pending_retrieval_intent is set AND gaps remain → set clarification_needed=True, build new clarification question, skip retrieval (Fix 2 — keep asking)
- intent == 'clarification_response' AND context.pending_retrieval_intent is None → no prior paused retrieval, skip to END with empty evidence
- has_gap AND retrieval intent → set clarification_needed=True, context.set_pending_retrieval_intent(intent) (Fix 1), skip retrieval
- retrieval intent AND context.is_ready_for_retrieval() → proceed to construct_retrieval_query node (enforces INV-DC-4)
- intent does not require retrieval (general_question) → skip retrieval entirely

3e. construct_retrieval_query node: user message (primary) + signal values (reinforcement) + domain anchor assembled into query_text. For clarification resumes, signal values only. School name excluded. top_k = _BASE_TOP_K + retrieval_attempts * _TOP_K_INCREMENT. Calls RetrievalQuery.from_context(session_id, query_text, intent, context_status) — enforces INV-RQ-1, INV-RQ-2, INV-RQ-3
3f. execute_retrieval node: calls IKnowledgeRetrievalService.retrieve(RetrievalQueryDTO) → RetrievedEvidenceDTO
3g. evaluate_evidence_quality node: calls IEvidenceEvaluator.evaluate(query, evidence) → ScoredRetrievedEvidence
— recall < threshold: refine query and retry execute_retrieval (enforces INV-RE-3)
— quality passes: proceed to return evidence (enforces INV-RE-4)
4. Call IDecisionContextRepository.save(context) — persist updated signals and state
5. Drain domain events (ContextUpdated, GapDetected, ContextReady — intra-component only)
6. Return TurnIntelligenceResult from final DecisionGraphState
invariants_enforced: INV-DC-4 — retrieval only activates when intent requires it AND context is ready or enriched
INV-DC-5 — signals accumulate monotonically; context.add_signal() never removes (domain-enforced)
INV-RQ-1 — query_text constructed from context.snapshot(), never from raw message
INV-RQ-2 — source_target derived from intent (product_inquiry → product_catalog; referral_question → referral_program_rules)
INV-RQ-3 — source_target validated against intent in RetrievalQuery.from_context() factory
INV-RE-1 — context_recall_score computed after every retrieval execution
INV-RE-2 — context_precision_score computed after every retrieval execution
INV-RE-3 — retrieval retried with refined query if recall falls below threshold
INV-RE-4 — evidence with failed scores does not exit this use case toward response generation
INV-WF-2 — WF2 (Product Recommendation) activates only when context is ready/enriched and intent is product_inquiry
use_cases.notes: The DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph is the execution engine wired inside this use case — its node/edge definitions are materialized in the Workflow Orchestration task (Task 2). This use case owns the transaction boundary: load DecisionContext → run subgraph → persist context → return result.

use_cases.name: ExecuteRetrievalUseCase
use_cases.trigger_workflow: WF2 (Product Recommendation) and WF3 (Referral Guidance) — called per retrieval execution
use_cases.purpose: Routes a retrieval query to the correct knowledge source based on source_target. Delegates to the appropriate vector store repository. Returns raw retrieved content chunks as RetrievedEvidenceDTO. Never invents or rewrites content — only returns what the knowledge store provides.

fields.name: query_text
fields.note: Enriched query text constructed by COMP-2 from context snapshot

fields.name: source_target
fields.note: product_catalog | referral_program_rules

output_response.name: RetrievedEvidenceDTO
output_response.note: DTO defined in the caller's (COMP-2) port location; mapped from domain retrieval result inside this use case

fields.name: chunks
fields.note: Top-matching content text from vector store

fields.name: evidence_type
fields.note: product_evidence | referral_rule_evidence

dependencies.role: semantic search over product_catalog collection when source_target=product_catalog
semantic search over referral_program_rules collection when source_target=referral_program_rules

execution_flow: 1. Validate source_target is one of: product_catalog, referral_program_rules — raise InvalidTargetError otherwise
2. Route on source_target:
2a. product_catalog: call IProductRepository.search(query_text) → list[Product] (enforces INV-AR-2)
2b. referral_program_rules: call IReferralRuleRepository.search(query_text) → list[ReferralRule] (enforces INV-AR-3)
3. Map retrieved entities to RetrievedEvidenceDTO:
- chunks: list of content text strings extracted from retrieved entities
- evidence_type: product_evidence | referral_rule_evidence based on source_target
4. Return RetrievedEvidenceDTO
invariants_enforced: INV-AR-2 — product recommendations grounded only in retrieved products (IProductRepository returns only existing catalog items)
INV-AR-3 — referral guidance grounded only in retrieved referral rules (IReferralRuleRepository returns only existing rules)
INV-AR-4 — this use case does not generate or rewrite content; it only retrieves
use_cases.notes: This is the leaf use case in the system dependency chain. COMP-3 has no outbound dependencies. It receives a query and returns raw content — quality evaluation (recall/precision scoring) happens in COMP-2, not here. This use case is invoked by KnowledgeRetrievalAdapter (shell) on behalf of COMP-2.

folder_structure.note: Use cases live in the application layer of each bounded context. Commands and responses are co-located with their use case file.

dtos_summary.note: Cross-context DTOs are positioned adjacent to the port interface they serve, in the caller's application layer.
